role: Deputy director of commerce at the Natanz enrichment facility; last victim of the 2010–2012 nuclear scientist assassination campaign
bio: Mostafa Ahmadi Roshan was an Iranian chemical engineer and deputy director of commerce at the Natanz uranium enrichment facility, operated by the [Atomic Energy Organisation of Iran](/orgs/atomic-energy-organisation-of-iran). On 11 January 2012, he was killed in Tehran when a magnetic bomb was placed on the side of his car by a motorcyclist as he drove to work on Shahid Golnabi Street in the Seyed Khandan district. His bodyguard-driver was also killed. Ahmadi Roshan was the last of four Iranian nuclear-linked scientists assassinated between 2010 and 2012 in the broader [2010–2012 nuclear scientist assassination campaign](/events/iran-nuclear-scientist-assassinations-2010). Western intelligence sources attributed the killing to Israeli intelligence (Mossad). Iran accused the United States and Israel.

sources.title: Fourth Iranian Nuclear Scientist Killed in Car Blast

editorial_notes: Killed 11 January 2012. Magnetic bomb on car. Deputy director of commerce at Natanz. Bodyguard also killed. Last in the 2010-2012 series.
